title: elymus trachycaulus subsp. teslinensis
intro: Elymus trachycaulus subsp. teslinensis is a subspecies of wild rye, a type of grass native to North America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Elymus trachycaulus subsp. teslinensis include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture for use in herbal medicine, and it is also sometimes smoked for its antiseptic properties.
